title: Generalized Model Fractional Quantum Hall States on Lattices
abstract: Model wave functions are essential for studying fractional quantum Hall phases, yet lattice model states have so far been limited to bosonic systems with on-site interactions. In this work, by combining analytical and numerical methods, we systematically construct lattice model states for the Laughlin, Moore--Read, and general $\mathbb{Z}_k$ Read--Rezayi series. Our lattice-specific states are characterized by their idealized energy and entanglement features and are distinguished from their continuum counterparts by a modified clustering behavior. Our theory advances the understanding of the stability of topologically ordered phases and illustrates the organizing principles of the conformal Hilbert space on lattices. Practically, this work paves the way for further studying lattice-specific excitations and offers a constructive route for engineering topological orders within density interactions, with potential immediate implications for cold-atom and synthetic flat-band platforms.
summary: This paper presents a systematic method to generalize continuum fractional quantum Hall wave functions to lattice systems by replacing contact clustering with discrete lattice-displaced zeros. The resulting model states for the Read-Rezayi series are exact ground states of specific density interactions and preserve essential topological features. This provides a blueprint for engineering topological phases in cold-atom and synthetic flat-band systems.
main_problem: The difficulty of constructing exact lattice model wave functions for fractional quantum Hall (FQH) states (like Laughlin, Moore-Read, and Read-Rezayi) beyond simple bosonic systems, specifically overcoming the limitation that particles on a lattice cannot be infinitesimally close to satisfy continuum clustering conditions.
main_result: The authors systematically construct lattice model states for the entire Read-Rezayi series by using a delta-deformation that replaces infinitesimal contact zeros with discrete, distributed lattice zeros. These states are proven to be exact gapped ground states of specific density-density interactions and retain the idealized entanglement features of their continuum counterparts.
method: The work combines analytical construction using elliptic functions (e.g., modified Weierstrass sigma functions) with numerical exact diagonalization on square and rectangular tori.
model_or_system: The study focuses on lattice-based synthetic platforms, such as the Kapit-Mueller model (a Hofstadter model with flat bands) and density-density interaction Hamiltonians where the interaction pattern is matched to the zeros of the wave function.
key_observables: Many-body energy spectrum, pair and three-particle correlation functions (g2, g3), particle-cut entanglement spectrum (PES), projected structure factor, and spectral flow under twisted boundary conditions.
important_parameters: Filling factor (nu = 1/3, 1/4, 1/2), interaction displacement (delta), magnetic flux density (phi), and interaction range.
main_assumptions: The construction is specific to 'lattice ideal bands' like the Kapit-Mueller model; the broader implications for non-ideal bands are not fully addressed.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows diagnostics for the nu=1/3 Laughlin state including the many-body spectrum, correlation function zeros, and entanglement gap; Figure 2 demonstrates phase transitions induced by increasing interaction range; Figure 3 shows diagnostics for non-Abelian states; Supplemental figures show spectral flow, bosonic states, and the nu=1/4 Moore-Read state.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of lattice FQH construction, presents the delta-deformation theory for Laughlin states, extends the construction to Moore-Read and Read-Rezayi series, analyzes phase transitions via interaction range, and provides detailed numerical verification in the supplemental material.
why_interesting: This work provides a constructive route for engineering topological orders in synthetic flat-band platforms like cold atoms, offering a way to realize complex non-Abelian states through controllable density interactions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2. Interaction-range-induced phase transitions. (a) Many-body spectrum at ν = ϕ = 1/3 for Ne = 8 with interaction displacement δ = 4a1. Additional low-energy states, highlighted by the red ellipse, appear at large |δ|, indicating the closing of the Laughlin many-body gap. (b) Corresponding particle entanglement spectrum for NA = 4. A finite principal entanglement gap opens, marked by the dotted line, which sets the cutoff used to identify the low- lying PES manifold. (c) Projected structure factor ¯S(q) of a representative zero-energy state, showing pronounced finite- momentum peaks. FIG. 1. Diagnostics of the ν = 1/3 δ-deformed lat- tice Laughlin state. (a) Many-body spectrum of Hδ for nearest-neighbor interacting fermions at ν = ϕ = 1/3 in the Kapit–Mueller flat band, computed on a 9 × 8 square lat- tice. Red dots mark the exactly degenerate threefold zero- energy ground-state manifold. (b) Pair correlation function g(2)(0, r) of one zero mode. The three exact zeros are marked by red crosses. (c) Momentum-resolved particle-cut entan- glement spectrum for subsystem size NA = 4.
